commands.review_summary:
The PR refactors dependency-change awareness into a checked-in trusted-base Node script, adds dependency graph hard-fail override logic, and updates CODEOWNERS plus workflow/script tests.

PR surface: Tests +168, Config -142, Other +520. Total +546 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings from source inspection. A package.json graph-field change outside dependencies/devDependencies is not included, the workflow passes only github.token to a team-membership check, and stale approvals can be accepted if the guard comment was deleted between graph-change runs.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Requires secops/maintainer judgment on the workflow authorization boundary plus targeted code fixes before merge; not a safe autonomous repair lane as-is.

target.title: feat(ci): guard dependency graph changes in PRs
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the inline dependency-awareness workflow logic with a checked-in Node script that runs from the trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`.
- Labels and comments only for actual dependency graph surfaces: package lockfiles, workspace/patch dependency policy files, or `package.json` `dependencies`/`devDependencies` field changes.
- Hard-fails package lockfile and dependency/devDependency manifest changes unless `@openclaw/openclaw-secops` authorizes the current guard state with `/allow-dependencies-change`.
- Keeps `dependencies-changed` as the only dependency label; the hard block is the failing check plus the guard comment.
- Adds secops ownership for the guard and focused workflow/script tests.

## Notes

- Script-only or metadata-only `package.json` changes do not get the dependency label and do not block.
- `/allow-dependencies-change` is only a comment token consumed on the next workflow run or manual re-run. It does not trigger an action or add labels by itself.
- The override records the current head SHA when accepted. After authorization, a later push requires a fresh `/allow-dependencies-change` comment.
- This PR adds the failing check behavior, but repository rulesets/branch protection still need to require the `Dependency Change Awareness` check before it becomes an enforced merge gate.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts`
